success_factors: Workflow completed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize) with overall status=success and no runtime errors/warnings.
Import-based adapter strategy was appropriate for pyproj (import feasibility 0.93), enabling rapid MCP wrapping without invasive code changes.
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio, indicating baseline operability of scaffold and entrypoint wiring.
Repository structure and key modules were correctly identified (CRS/Transformer/Geod/datadir/network/sync), supporting meaningful endpoint exposure.
failure_reasons: Quality/data gaps remain: original project tests were not executed (passed=false, execution_time=0), MCP tests are effectively empty, and key metrics (LOC, file size, CPU/memory/perf) are zero/unknown.
DeepWiki integration failed, reducing architecture-context depth for generation/review.
Endpoint list contains duplicates and potential ambiguity (e.g., transformer/transformergroup/get_data_dir repeated).

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; repository imported via zip fallback (73 files). Exact node timing not provided, but no download instability indicated.
node_performance.analysis_time: Successful static analysis with medium complexity and low intrusiveness risk; entry-point detection appears incomplete despite known CLI module.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation completed quickly enough within total 282.92s, producing 7 files; however generated LOC/size telemetry reported as 0, limiting quantitative assessment.
node_performance.test_time: Run node produced healthy MCP startup, but substantive automated tests were not executed (project tests empty; MCP contract coverage absent).
